tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function, and high urine protein and ketones, indicating possible nephropathy and ketosis. Urine is abnormal in appearance, color, and specific gravity, supporting renal involvement. Low HbA1c suggests possible anemia or recent hypoglycemia. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend further renal assessment and clinical correlation for underlying causes, including dehydration, acute kidney injury, or chronic kidney disease.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
